mentions.note: Beef cost inflation is real pressure, but 2.6% yield, great reputation, 21x earnings with room to 18–19x. DCA strategy: buy at $280, $279, $270, $265, and 50 shares at $260 to build better cost basis.
Missed quarter is rare; unlikely to repeat. Fast food fortress beaten down; recovery play.
Selling at 21x earnings, breaking down; 2.7% yield acceptable; want to buy on pullback if yield hits 3%; QSR better company but MCD buyable on weakness.
Reports Thursday; always surprises to upside; value package very popular; competition less effective; stock drifting lower but fundamentals solid—buy it.
Down $10 today; fantastic quarter; preferable to Wendy's in quick-service restaurant space.

Up 9% YTD at all-time high. Crushed it: global same-store sales +5.7% (beat 3.9% expect), US +6.8%, 10% revenue growth, 10% EPS growth. Value strategy ($5 meals, McValue mix-and-match, extra value meals) winning back lower-income customers. 2,600 new locations planned; targeting 50k by end 2027. Simple AI-proof story. Cramer still loving it; own don't trade.

Still loving it; value strategy ($ meals, McValue platform) proving durable; 5.7% global comps, 6.8% US comps, 10% revenue growth, 10% earnings growth; expanding to 50K locations by 2027; immune to AI disruption; simple story for money managers.
Hurt by beef supply issues but value proposition back; tariff cut on Argentine beef positive; reports Wednesday.
Likes the franchise but can't pound table given elevated food inflation backdrop.
